    Notifications
      Today

    1. Watch: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's house was reportedly the target of a Molotov cocktail attack on Friday. Yahoo Finance's Dan Howley explains.

    2. Stocks post second straight winning week amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire

    3. US tariff revenue dropped by over $4 billion in March. It's down almost 30% since October.

    4. US stocks open higher after March CPI inflation data shows energy surge

    5. Inflation soars by most since 2022 as gas prices bite

    6. Americans close to retirement are saving for later, but they're worried about getting by right now

    7. Government data out Friday will show how much consumer prices rose in March — and forecasts point to soaring inflation.

    8. Palantir plunges after 'Big Short' investor Michael Burry says Anthropic is eating its lunch

    9. Stocks rebounded to extend their winning streak, while oil rose ahead of US-Iran talks

    10. Stocks turned positive after Israel agreed to talks with Lebanon, reviving optimism for the US-Iran ceasefire

    11. US stocks pull back, oil surges as US-Iran ceasefire looks increasingly shaky

    12. Sticky reading on inflation pre-war will keep Fed on hold

    13. The New York Times may have found bitcoin founder Satoshi Nakamoto. Here's what you need to know.

    14. Fed officials saw risks of the Iran war increasing inflation and delaying rate cuts in a March meeting

    15. The Strait of Hormuz has immediately become the first major flash point of Trump's 'fragile' ceasefire

    16. US stocks surged while oil prices plunged as relief from a fragile US-Iran ceasefire rippled through markets

    17. US stocks soar, with Dow surging nearly 1,400 points as Iran truce restarts Hormuz traffic

    18. Delta beats on Q1 earnings, maintains guidance despite fuel spike, TSA headwinds

    19. Investors are turning to social media and following 'finfluencers' to inform their investment decisions

    20. High new car prices, rising gas costs, and rising interest rates brought buyers to used car lots

    21. Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq futures soar after Trump calls for two-week ceasefire

    22. Markets weigh the chances of economic fallout from an attack on Iran against previous threats from Trump

    23. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq ended the day slightly higher on hopes of an Iran deadline extension

    24. Anthropic launches cybersecurity partnership with Amazon, Apple, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, and others

    25. Tech is doing everything right and getting left behind. Here's what could turn that around.

    26. US stocks drift lower as Trump's deadline for Iran ultimatum looms

    27. US gas prices risk topping $5 per gallon if the Strait of Hormuz stays closed, JPMorgan says

    28. Netflix stands to get richer after losing Warner Bros. bidding war. Here's why.

    29. Goldman Sachs delivered a warning to laid-off tech workers that it will take more time to find a new job than expected

    30. The Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq gained ahead of Trump's deadline for an Iran deal

    31. US stocks open muted, oil dips as Wall Street eyes push for Iran truce talks

    32. JPMorgan CEO flags Iran war-fueled inflation, private credit, and AI-linked job losses as risks to the economy

    33. 'Wouldn't be shocked' if bull market continues: Wall Street aims to look past Iran conflict

    34. What if I can’t pay my taxes? 5 ways to tackle your bill.

    35. The US labor market right now can be defined by one word: Whiplash

    36. The Trump administration has signaled it will offer broad tariff refunds. That could mean millions for companies.

    37. Inflation readings, oil volatility, and airline earnings: What to watch this week

    38. Britain's billion-dollar bet on a hypercar is "a genuinely incredible machine to drive," writes senior reporter Pras Subramanian.

    39. Cocoa prices are falling — but not in time for Easter (CNN Business)

    40. 7 things to know about Schwab's new brokerage account for teenagers

    41. The war in Iran shows no signs of slowing down, and its damage may be felt for years to come

    42. The best markets for first-time homebuyers are increasingly in these two regions of the US

    43. The company's gravitational pull has the potential to elevate Elon Musk as a government partner, Wall Street fixture, and tech entrepreneur.
      44. Older

    44. US judge upholds block of subpoenas in criminal investigation of Jay Powell (Financial Times)

    45. Hyundai's Boulder concept previews the automaker's truck future, built in America

    46. What we've learned in a year since 'Liberation Day'

    47. Americans nearing retirement plan to take Social Security 'as soon as eligible'

    48. March jobs report: US economy adds 178,000 jobs, unemployment rate falls to 4.3% in surprise turnaround

    49. The March jobs report will show a fuller picture of the state of the job market, just as the Iran war began to weigh on the global economy.

    50. Jet fuel prices are up 100%, oil prices keep surging, and airlines are paying the price

          NasdaqGS - Nasdaq Real Time Price • USD

          lululemon athletica inc. (LULU)

          Is LULU a long-term buy?
          163.86 -2.65 (-1.59%)
          At close: April 10 at 4:00:01 PM EDT
          163.80 -0.06 (-0.04%)
          After hours: 7:59:17 PM EDT
          Is LULU a long-term buy?

          Financials Lululemon Athletica Inc's financial performance shows mixed trends across various metrics in FY 2025 Q3. Profitability metrics like gross margin and operating margin have declined slightly, indicating potential pressure on margins. Meanwhile, efficiency metrics such as inventory turnover and cash conversion cycle have worsened, suggesting challenges in inventory management and cash flow. However, EPS growth remains positive, indicating some resilience in earnings despite broader challenges.

          Lululemon Athletica Inc's financial performance shows mixed trends across various metrics in FY 2025 Q3. Profitability metrics like gross margin and operating margin have declined slightly, indicating potential pressure on margins. Meanwhile, efficiency metrics such as inventory turnover and cash conversion cycle have worsened, suggesting challenges in inventory management and cash flow. However, EPS growth remains positive, indicating some resilience in earnings despite broader challenges.

          • 3 Data derived from multiple sources or calculated by Yahoo Finance.
          • 4 Data provided by Morningstar, Inc.
          • 5 Shares outstanding is taken from the most recently filed quarterly or annual report and Market Cap is calculated using shares outstanding.
          • 6 Implied Shares Outstanding of common equity, assuming the conversion of all convertible subsidiary equity into common.
          • 7 EBITDA is calculated by S&P Global Market Intelligence using methodology that may differ from that used by a company in its reporting.
          • 8 A company's float is a measure of the number of shares available for trading by the public. It's calculated by taking the number of issued and outstanding shares minus any restricted stock, which may not be publicly traded.

          Abbreviation Guide

          • mrq = Most Recent Quarter
          • ttm = Trailing Twelve Months
          • yoy = Year Over Year
          • lfy = Last Fiscal Year
          • fye = Fiscal Year Ending
          See Statistics Help for definitions of terms used.
